name: recruit-ai-mcp
description: Bootstrap fork for recruiter/search-firm intelligence. The current tool surface still uses inherited deal-intel tools while storage, config, and MongoDB defaults are isolated for recruit-ai-mcp.
long_description: recruit-ai-mcp is a fork of deal-intel-mcp for live-testing a recruiter/search-firm second brain. This bootstrap release keeps the inherited deal-intel Python module and MCP tools while isolating public metadata, config paths, runtime env variables, and MongoDB defaults for recruit-ai-mcp. Later work will replace the deal-centric tool surface with candidate, client-company, position, feedback, and recommendation workflows.

tools.name: config_doctor
tools.description: Diagnose profile, storage, vector search, and LLM readiness without writes or LLM calls.

tools.name: get_tool_catalog
tools.description: List the current Deal Intelligence tool surface when host tool search shows only a truncated subset.

tools.name: update_config
tools.description: Preview or apply safe non-secret user-config changes such as LLM provider/model, report output directory, timezone, and tool surface.

tools.name: add_product_context_note
tools.description: Dry-run-first local writer for pasted seller-side product and solution notes before product-context indexing.

tools.name: index_product_context
tools.description: Dry-run-first local RAG cache builder for seller-side product and solution documents.

tools.name: get_product_context
tools.description: Retrieve bounded seller-side product context snippets from the local RAG cache.

tools.name: get_qualification_templates
tools.description: List built-in qualification framework templates such as MEDDPICC, simple B2B, pilot/PoC, enterprise procurement, and product-led sales.

tools.name: validate_qualification_framework
tools.description: Validate a candidate qualification framework template or JSON/YAML payload without writing config.

tools.name: update_qualification_framework
tools.description: Dry-run-first config update for custom qualification frameworks; built-in presets are immutable and can be copied with copy_as_key.

tools.name: list_qualification_frameworks
tools.description: List built-in and saved qualification frameworks and show which framework is active.

tools.name: set_active_qualification_framework
tools.description: Dry-run-first config update that switches the active qualification framework without recomputing existing deals.

tools.name: delete_qualification_framework
tools.description: Dry-run-first deletion for stored custom qualification frameworks; built-ins and active frameworks are protected.

tools.name: backfill_qualification
tools.description: Dry-run-first recompute of current qualification snapshots from stored evidence without raw-content reads or LLM calls.

tools.name: backfill_qualification_reextract
tools.description: Dry-run-first maintenance path that re-extracts active-framework evidence from historical interaction content and may call the configured LLM in apply mode.

tools.name: create_deal
tools.description: Create a new deal for a prospect company.

tools.name: add_meeting
tools.description: Deprecated developer-surface alias for add_interaction with interaction_type=meeting.

tools.name: add_interaction
tools.description: Add a meeting, email, interview, call summary, or internal note with source-aware active-framework qualification extraction; MEDDPICC is the default.

tools.name: update_stage
tools.description: Move a deal to a new pipeline stage and log it to stage history.

tools.name: update_deal
tools.description: Update confirmed deal value and selected metadata fields.

tools.name: archive_deal
tools.description: Archive a deal so default BI/read paths hide it without hard deletion.

tools.name: restore_deal
tools.description: Restore an archived deal back into default BI/read paths.

tools.name: delete_deal
tools.description: Hard-delete an archived deal after a dry-run preview and safe audit snapshot.

tools.name: migrate_local_data
tools.description: Dry-run-first migration of user-created local personal deals into MongoDB.

tools.name: create_sample_data
tools.description: Create fictional onboarding sample deals in a separate demo database.

tools.name: delete_sample_data
tools.description: Delete fictional onboarding sample deals from the separate demo database.

tools.name: get_deal
tools.description: Retrieve a safe deal record with interaction history and active-framework qualification scores, excluding raw notes, raw content, contacts, and embeddings.

tools.name: get_deal_raw
tools.description: Developer-only raw deal read that requires confirmed_by_user, a reason, and include_raw_content while still excluding embeddings.

tools.name: list_deals
tools.description: List deals with health_pct, gaps, and stuck flag, optionally filtered by stage.

tools.name: get_insights
tools.description: Run BI aggregation queries: pipeline_overview / win_patterns / loss_patterns / compare_won_lost / gap_frequency / industry_benchmark / stage_velocity.

tools.name: get_metrics
tools.description: Return pipeline_health or pipeline_trend metrics from the shared BI calculators.

tools.name: get_deal_gaps
tools.description: Return prioritized read-only sales follow-up gaps for deals.

tools.name: get_deal_review
tools.description: Return evidence-aware deal review output for one deal without raw notes, contacts, or embeddings.

tools.name: get_usage
tools.description: Summarize persisted server-side LLM token usage and safe cost estimates without exposing prompts or secrets.

tools.name: export_report
tools.description: Export human-facing weekly_pipeline or pipeline_trend report artifacts.

tools.name: export_data
tools.description: Export spreadsheet-ready CSV datasets such as open_deals, all_deals, closed_deals, or hubspot_deals without raw notes or LLM calls.

tools.name: get_user_memory
tools.description: Read constrained user-memory Markdown files for assistant context loading.

tools.name: record_user_memory
tools.description: Append durable user feedback to safe user-memory Markdown files after secret/path checks.

tools.name: get_customer_themes
tools.description: Rank recurring customer concerns by unique deal count; the first step in the customer-theme workflow.

tools.name: get_customer_theme_breakdown
tools.description: Compare customer themes by stage, primary industry, industry tag, or dimension after ranking.

tools.name: get_customer_theme_evidence
tools.description: Return privacy-safe evidence snippets for one known customer theme key.

tools.name: search_deals
tools.description: Semantic search - find deals similar to a natural language query (M0 uses local Python cosine; Atlas search is optional on M10+).

tools.name: analyze_deal
tools.description: Optional server-side LLM tool that previews BD strategy recommendations by default and persists only after explicit confirmation.

python_path.title: Python interpreter path
python_path.description: Path to the Python interpreter where recruit-ai-mcp is installed. Prefer the Python path printed by `npx recruit-ai-mcp setup`.

storage_backend.title: Storage backend
storage_backend.description: Use mongo for real Atlas-backed data (default), or local_sample only for zero-config sample/local personal mode.

tools_surface.title: MCP tool surface
tools_surface.description: Use auto for profile-based filtering, sample for zero-config/local personal tools, standard for normal real-data operation, or developer for all tools.

reporting_language.title: Report language
reporting_language.description: Language for generated Markdown reports. Use en for English or ko for Korean. CSV contracts stay unchanged.

mongodb_uri.title: MongoDB Atlas URI
mongodb_uri.description: Connection string for your MongoDB Atlas cluster. Required only when storage backend is mongo. M0 free tier works for full mode.

llm_provider.title: LLM provider
llm_provider.description: Provider to use: chatgpt_oauth, anthropic, or openai_api. This field overrides the legacy ChatGPT OAuth checkbox.

use_chatgpt_oauth.title: Use ChatGPT Plus/Pro subscription (legacy)
use_chatgpt_oauth.description: Legacy provider selector. Prefer LLM provider above. When LLM provider is blank, true uses ChatGPT OAuth and false uses Anthropic.

anthropic_api_key.title: Anthropic API key
anthropic_api_key.description: Leave blank if using ChatGPT OAuth above. Can also be set via ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in the repo's .env file.

openai_api_key.title: OpenAI API key
openai_api_key.description: Required only when LLM provider is openai_api. Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY in the repo's .env file.
